questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $a_1 x+b_1 y+c_1 z+d_1=0$ and $a_2 x+b_2 y+c_2 z+d_2=0$ be two planes, where $d_1, d_2\gt0$. Then origin lies in acute angle if $a_1 a_2+b_1 b_2+c_1 c_2 \lt 0$ and origin lies in obtuse angle if $a_1 a_2+b_1 b_2+c_1 c_2\gt0$. Further point $\left(x_1, y_1, z_1\right)$ and origin both lie either in acute angle or in obtuse angle, if $\left(a_1 x_1+b_1 y_1+c_1 z_1+d_1\right)$ $\left(a_2 x_1+b_2 y_1+c_2 z_1+d_2\right)\gt0$. One of $\left(x_1, y_1, z_1\right)$ and origin lie in acute angle and the other in obtuse angle, if $\left(a_1 x_1+b_1 y_1+c_1 z_1+d_1\right)\left(a_2 x_1+b_2 y_1+c_2 z_1+d_2\right) \lt 0$Question: Given the planes $2 x+3 y-4 z+7=0$ and $x-2 y+3 z-5=0$, if a point $P$ is $(1,-2,3)$, then
options: $O$ and $P$ both lie in acute angle between the planes
O and P both lie in obtuse angle
O lies in acute angle, P lies in obtuse angle
O lies in obtuse angle, P lies an acute angle
questions.solution:
Equation of the second plane is $-x+2 y-3 z+5=0$
$2(-1)+3,2+(-4)(-3)\gt0$
$\therefore$ origin lies in obtuse angle $(2 \times 1+3(-2)-4 \times 3+7)(-1+2(-2)-3 \times 3+5)$
$=(2-6-12+7)(-1-4-9+5)\gt0$
$\therefore \mathrm{P}$ lies in obtuse angle
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph The base of a pyramid is rectangular, three of its vertices of the base are $\mathrm{A}(2,2,-1), \mathrm{B}(3,1,2)$ and $\mathrm{C}(1,1,1)$ (may or may not be in order). Its vertex at the top is $\mathrm{P}\left(4,-\frac{26}{3},-\frac{10}{3}\right)$ and fourth vertex of the base is D. On the basis of above information, answer the following :Question Co-ordinates of foot of the normal drawn from P on the base of the pyramid are -
questions.solution:
Equation of the base
$a(x-1)+b(y-1)+c(z-1)=0$
which also passes through $\mathrm{A}(2,2,-1)$ and
$\mathrm{B}(3,1,2)$
$\therefore a+b-2 c=0 ...(i)$
$2 a+c=0...(ii)$
$\begin{aligned} & c=-2 a, b=-5 a \\ & a(x-1)-5 a(y-1)-2 a(z-1)=0\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow \quad x-5 y-2 z+6=0$
foot of the normal from P
$\frac{x-4}{1}=\frac{y+\frac{26}{3}}{-5}=\frac{z+\frac{10}{3}}{-2}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{x-4}{1}=\frac{y+\frac{26}{3}}{-5}=\frac{z+\frac{10}{3}}{-2}=-2$
$\Rightarrow \quad x=2, \quad y=\frac{4}{3}, \quad z=\frac{2}{3}$
$\therefore \quad\left(2, \frac{4}{3}, \frac{2}{3}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph The base of a pyramid is rectangular, three of its vertices of the base are $\mathrm{A}(2,2,-1), \mathrm{B}(3,1,2)$ and $\mathrm{C}(1,1,1)$ (may or may not be in order). Its vertex at the top is $\mathrm{P}\left(4,-\frac{26}{3},-\frac{10}{3}\right)$ and fourth vertex of the base is D. On the basis of above information, answer the following :Question Volume of the pyramid is -
questions.solution:
Volume of the pyramid
$=\frac{1}{3}($ Base area $) \times$ Height $=\frac{1}{3}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}}| \times \mathrm{EP}$
$=\frac{1}{3}|\hat{\mathrm{i}}-5 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}| \times \sqrt{120}$
$=\frac{1}{3} \sqrt{30} \cdot \sqrt{120}=20$ cubic units
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $a_1 x+b_1 y+c_1 z+d_1=0$ and $a_2 x+b_2 y+c_2 z+d_2=0$ be two planes, where $d_1, d_2\gt0$. Then origin lies in acute angle if $a_1 a_2+b_1 b_2+c_1 c_2 \lt 0$ and origin lies in obtuse angle if $a_1 a_2+b_1 b_2+c_1 c_2\gt0$. Further point $\left(x_1, y_1, z_1\right)$ and origin both lie either in acute angle or in obtuse angle, if $\left(a_1 x_1+b_1 y_1+c_1 z_1+d_1\right)$ $\left(a_2 x_1+b_2 y_1+c_2 z_1+d_2\right)\gt0$. One of $\left(x_1, y_1, z_1\right)$ and origin lie in acute angle and the other in obtuse angle, if $\left(a_1 x_1+b_1 y_1+c_1 z_1+d_1\right)\left(a_2 x_1+b_2 y_1+c_2 z_1+d_2\right) \lt 0$Question: Given the planes $x+2 y-3 z+5=0$ and $2 x+y+3 z+1=0$. If a point $P$ is $(2,-1,2)$, then
options: O and P both lie in acute angle between the planes
$O$ and $P$ both lie in obtuse angle
$O$ lies in acute angle, $P$ lies in obtuse angle
O lies in obtuse angle, P lies an acute angle
questions.solution:
$1 \times 2+2 \times 1-3 \times 3 \lt 0$
$\therefore$ Origin lies in acute angle
Also $(2+2(-1)-3(2)+5)(2 \times 2-1+3 \times 2+1)=(-1)(10) \lt 0$
$\therefore$ P lies in obtuse angle
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: Paragraph: Let $a_1 x+b_1 y+c_1 z+d_1=0$ and $a_2 x+b_2 y+c_2 z+d_2=0$ be two planes, where $d_1, d_2\gt0$. Then origin lies in acute angle if $a_1 a_2+b_1 b_2+c_1 c_2 \lt 0$ and origin lies in obtuse angle if $a_1 a_2+b_1 b_2+c_1 c_2\gt0$. Further point $\left(x_1, y_1, z_1\right)$ and origin both lie either in acute angle or in obtuse angle, if $\left(a_1 x_1+b_1 y_1+c_1 z_1+d_1\right)$ $\left(a_2 x_1+b_2 y_1+c_2 z_1+d_2\right)\gt0$. One of $\left(x_1, y_1, z_1\right)$ and origin lie in acute angle and the other in obtuse angle, if $\left(a_1 x_1+b_1 y_1+c_1 z_1+d_1\right)\left(a_2 x_1+b_2 y_1+c_2 z_1+d_2\right) \lt 0$Question: Given the planes $x+2 y-3 z+2=0$ and $x-2 y+3 z+7=0$, if the point $P$ is $(1,2,2)$, then
options: O and P both lie in acute angle between the planes
O and P both lie in obtuse angle
O lies in acute angle, P lies in obtuse angle
O lies in obtuse angle, P lies an acute angle
questions.solution:
$1-4-9 \lt 0$
$\therefore$ Origin lies in acute angle
Further $(1+4-6+2)(1-4+6+7)\gt0$
$\therefore$ The point P lies in acute angle
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph Let $f(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{ae}^{2 \mathrm{x}}+\mathrm{be}^{\mathrm{x}}+\mathrm{cx}$ satisfies the condition $f(0)=-1, f^{\prime}(\ln 2)=31$ and $\int_0^{\ln 4}(f(\mathrm{x})-\mathrm{cx}) \mathrm{dx}=\frac{39}{2}$. Image of a line $\frac{x-1}{3}=\frac{y-3}{5}=\frac{z-4}{2}$ in the plane $(a-3) x+(b+a) y+z+c=0$ be a line $L=0$ whose direction ratios are $1, \mathrm{p}, \mathrm{q}$ respectively. Let $\vec{\ell}=\mathrm{a} \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\mathrm{b} \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\mathrm{c} \hat{\mathrm{k}}$ and $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{m}}=(\mathrm{p}-2) \hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+(\mathrm{q}+3) \hat{\mathrm{k}}$. On the basis of above information, answer the following :Question $a+b+c$ is equal to -
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& f(x)=a e^{2 x}+b e^x+c x \\
& f(0)=a+b=-1...(i) \\
& f^{\prime}(x)=2 a e^{2 x}+b e^x+c \\
& f^{\prime}(\ell \ln 2)=8 a+2 b+c=31...(ii)
\end{aligned}$
$\int_0^{\ln 4}\left(a e^{2 x}+b e^x\right) d x=\left(a \frac{e^2 x}{2}+b e^x\right)_0^{\ln 4}$
$=8 a+4 b-\frac{a}{2}-b \quad \Rightarrow \quad \frac{15 a}{2}+3 b=\frac{39}{2}$
$\Rightarrow 15 a+6 b=39...(iii)$
Solving (i), (ii) \& (iii) we get
$\begin{aligned}
& a=5, b=-6, c=3 \\
& a+b+c=5-6+3=2
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: Consider a three dimensional Cartesian system with origin at O and three rectangular coordinate axes x , $y$ and $z$-axis. Suppose that the distance between two points $P$ and $Q$ in the space having their coordinates $\left(\mathrm{x}_1, \mathrm{y}_1, \mathrm{z}_1\right)$ and $\left(\mathrm{x}_2, \mathrm{y}_2, \mathrm{z}_2\right)$ respectively be defined by the following formula $$ d(P, Q)=\left|x_2-x_1\right|+\left|y_2-y_1\right|+\left|z_2-z_1\right| $$ Although the formula of distance between two points has been defined in a new way, yet the other definitions remain same (like section formula, direction cosines etc.). So, in general equations of straight line in space, plane in space remain unchanged. Now answer the following questions.Question: Locus of point $P$ if $d(O, P)=k$, where $k$ is a positive constant number, represents
options: a set of eight planes forming an octahedron
a set of eight planes forming hexagonal prism
an infinite cylinder of radius k
questions.solution: $d(O, P)=k$ $$ \Rightarrow|x|+|y|+|z|=k $$ which represents a set of 9 planes making intercepts of lengths $k$ on positive as well negative sides of all three axes. See the adjacent figure.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: Consider a three dimensional Cartesian system with origin at O and three rectangular coordinate axes x , $y$ and $z$-axis. Suppose that the distance between two points $P$ and $Q$ in the space having their coordinates $\left(\mathrm{x}_1, \mathrm{y}_1, \mathrm{z}_1\right)$ and $\left(\mathrm{x}_2, \mathrm{y}_2, \mathrm{z}_2\right)$ respectively be defined by the following formula $$ d(P, Q)=\left|x_2-x_1\right|+\left|y_2-y_1\right|+\left|z_2-z_1\right| $$ Although the formula of distance between two points has been defined in a new way, yet the other definitions remain same (like section formula, direction cosines etc.). So, in general equations of straight line in space, plane in space remain unchanged. Now answer the following questions.Question: Let $O A B C$ represents a regular tetrahedron with edge length $2 a$ and vertices $A, B, C$ lying on $x, y$ and $z$-axis respectively. Let $P$ be a point inside the region of the tetrahedron satisfying $d(O, P) \leq \min \{d(P, A), d(P, B), d(P, C)\}$. The maximum value of $d(O, P)$ is
questions.solution: The maximum value of $d(O, P)=$ circumradius of sphere $=a$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
